Active Work Architecture
─[ Gitignored Nav Drift ]─ ┐

name: gitignored-nav-drift description: CI nav checks fail when gitignored dirs are created at runtime by sticky files trigger: adding gitignored directories that appear in generated nav or config user-invocable: false

Gitignored Nav Drift

When a directory is gitignored but created at runtime (e.g., sticky file scaffolding), nav generators include it on dev machines but not on fresh clones. This causes CI staleness checks to fail.

The Problem

  1. docs/knowledge/local/ is gitignored
  2. ensureStickyFiles() creates it on session start
  3. generate-nav.sh includes it in mkdocs.yml (it exists locally)
  4. CI clones fresh — directory doesn’t exist — generator produces different output
  5. git diff --exit-code mkdocs.yml fails

Fix

In CI, create the runtime-scaffolded directories before running the nav check:

- name: Nav staleness check
  run: |
    node -e "require('./lib/banner').ensureStickyFiles('.')"
    bash scripts/generate-nav.sh && git diff --exit-code mkdocs.yml

General Rule

Any CI check that validates “generated file matches committed file” must first reproduce the same runtime environment as development. If sticky files or runtime scaffolding create directories that affect generation, CI must create them too.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
